constraints.expression: signers.every((s, i, arr) => arr.findIndex(o => o.signer_id == s.signer_id) == i)
constraints.message: Each signer_id MUST be unique within signers[]; duplicate signer_id entries create ambiguous key resolution at consumption time.
constraints.evaluation_note: Library-evaluated structural uniqueness check on signer_id within Keyring.signers[]. Bridge finding F-003 raised this as a missing guard: TypeBox / JSON Schema 2020-12 has no first-class uniqueItems-by-key primitive, so the constraint is enforced by the cross-field validator registered for Keyring (src/validators/index.ts). Failures fail-closed at error severity. The DSL expression form uses .every() + .findIndex() (both supported in constraint-DSL grammar v2.0) so cross-language runners (Go/Python/Rust) can re-implement the check identically; the library evaluator runs the equivalent logic in the cross-field validator.

constraints.expression: signers.every((s, i, arr) => arr.findIndex(o => o.key_ref == s.key_ref) == i)
constraints.message: Two SignerEntry rows referencing the same key material is typically a misconfiguration; if intentional (rotation overlap), set distinct signer_ids and document the window.
constraints.evaluation_note: Library-evaluated soft uniqueness on key_ref. Distinct signer_id (KR-1) is hard-required; key_ref duplication is soft-warned because legitimate rotation overlap windows DO exist (active + retiring sharing the same backing key during a brief transition). Surfaced as a warning so consumers can decide whether their integration treats overlap as intentional or as drift. Cross-runner authors implement the same check; the library evaluator runs the equivalent logic in the cross-field validator registered for Keyring.
